name: OWASP Top 10 for Large Language Model Applications

controls.title: Prompt Injection
controls.description: User or third-party inputs alter the model's behavior or output in unintended ways, including direct and indirect (data-borne) injection.

controls.title: Sensitive Information Disclosure
controls.description: The model exposes sensitive data — PII, credentials, proprietary or internal information — through its outputs.

controls.title: Supply Chain
controls.description: Vulnerabilities in third-party models, datasets, plugins, or components compromise integrity of the LLM application.

controls.title: Data and Model Poisoning
controls.description: Training, fine-tuning, or embedding data is manipulated to introduce backdoors, bias, or degraded behavior.

controls.title: Improper Output Handling
controls.description: Insufficient validation or encoding of model output before it reaches downstream systems (e.g. XSS, SQLi, SSRF, code execution).

controls.title: Excessive Agency
controls.description: Excessive functionality, permissions, or autonomy granted to an LLM-based system enables damaging actions.

controls.title: System Prompt Leakage
controls.description: System prompts or instructions containing sensitive configuration or secrets are exposed to users.

controls.title: Vector and Embedding Weaknesses
controls.description: Weaknesses in how vectors and embeddings are generated, stored, or retrieved (e.g. in RAG) enable injection, poisoning, or leakage across tenants.

controls.title: Misinformation
controls.description: The model produces false or misleading information presented as fact, including confabulation/hallucination, leading to harm when relied upon.

controls.title: Unbounded Consumption
controls.description: Uncontrolled resource use (compute, tokens, cost) via denial-of-wallet or denial-of-service against the model or its host.
